note: Simplified reference summaries. For full code text, consult ICC at https://codes.iccsafe.org/

101.title: Scope and Administration
101.summary: General scope of the IBC, applicability, and administrative provisions.

subsections.101.2: Scope - Applies to construction, alteration, relocation, enlargement, replacement, repair, equipment, use and occupancy, location, maintenance, removal and demolition of every building or structure
subsections.101.3: Intent - To establish minimum requirements to provide a reasonable level of safety, public health and general welfare

202.title: Definitions
202.summary: Definitions of terms used throughout the IBC.
key_terms: BUILDING - Any structure used for support or shelter
DWELLING UNIT - Single unit providing complete independent living facilities
FIRE AREA - Aggregate floor area enclosed and bounded by fire walls
STORY - Portion of a building between upper surface of a floor and upper surface of floor next above
GRADE PLANE - Reference plane for measuring building height
HIGH-RISE BUILDING - Building with occupied floor more than 75 ft above fire dept access

302.title: Classification of Occupancy
302.summary: Building use classifications including Assembly (A), Business (B), Educational (E), Factory (F), High-Hazard (H), Institutional (I), Mercantile (M), Residential (R), Storage (S), and Utility (U).

subsections.302.1: Occupancy Classification - Buildings shall be classified by occupancy group
subsections.303: Assembly Group A - A-1 through A-5
subsections.307: High-Hazard Group H - H-1 through H-5
subsections.308: Institutional Group I - I-1 through I-4
subsections.310: Residential Group R - R-1 through R-4

403.title: High-Rise Buildings
403.summary: Special requirements for buildings with occupied floors more than 75 feet above the lowest level of fire department vehicle access.

subsections.403.1: Applicability - Buildings with occupied floor more than 75 ft above lowest fire dept access

405.title: Underground Buildings
405.summary: Special requirements for underground buildings with occupied floors more than 30 feet below the lowest level of exit discharge.

subsections.405.1: Applicability - Buildings with floor level used for human occupancy more than 30 ft below exit discharge
subsections.405.2: Construction type - Type I construction required
subsections.405.3: Compartmentation - Max 1,500 sq ft smoke compartments required
subsections.405.4: Fire suppression - NFPA 13 automatic sprinklers required
subsections.405.7: Standby power required for smokeproof enclosures, ventilation, fire alarm, elevators
subsections.405.8: Emergency power required for emergency lighting, exit signs

406.title: Motor-Vehicle-Related Occupancies
406.summary: Requirements for parking garages, repair garages, and motor fuel-dispensing facilities.

subsections.406.4: Parking garages - Height and area increases for open parking
subsections.406.6: Hydrogen motor fuel-dispensing and generation facilities

503.title: General Building Height and Area Limitations
503.summary: Tables and requirements for maximum building height and floor area based on occupancy and construction type.

subsections.503.1: General height and area - See Table 504.3, 504.4, 506.2
subsections.504: Building height in feet and stories
subsections.506: Building area modifications (frontage and sprinkler increases)

602.title: Construction Classification
602.summary: Five types of construction: Type I (Fire-Resistive), Type II (Non-Combustible), Type III (Ordinary), Type IV (Heavy Timber/Mass Timber), Type V (Wood Frame).

subsections.602.1: General - Buildings classified by construction type
subsections.602.2: Type I - Noncombustible structural elements, fire-resistive
subsections.602.4: Type III - Exterior walls noncombustible, interior any material
subsections.602.5: Type IV - Heavy Timber and Mass Timber (IV-A, IV-B, IV-C, IV-HT)
subsections.602.6: Type V - Structural elements any material permitted by code

702.title: Fire-Resistance-Rated Construction - Definitions
702.summary: Definitions related to fire-resistance-rated construction elements.

subsections.702.1: Definitions - Fire barrier, fire partition, fire wall, shaft enclosure, horizontal assembly, smoke barrier, smoke partition

703.title: Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire Tests
703.summary: Methods for determining fire-resistance ratings of building elements.

subsections.703.1: Alternative methods - Testing (ASTM E119), calculations, prescriptive design
subsections.703.2: Fire-resistance ratings - Based on ASTM E119 standard fire test
subsections.703.4: Nonsymmetrical protection - Rating based on weaker side

706.title: Fire Walls
706.summary: Requirements for fire walls used to create separate buildings for height and area purposes.

subsections.706.1: General - Fire walls create separate buildings
subsections.706.2: Structural stability - Independent structural frame each side
subsections.706.4: Fire-resistance rating - 2hr to 4hr depending on occupancy
subsections.706.5: Horizontal continuity - Extend to exterior walls
subsections.706.6: Vertical continuity - Extend from foundation to or through roof

707.title: Fire Barriers
707.summary: Requirements for fire barriers used to separate occupancies, exit enclosures, and other fire-rated separations.

subsections.707.1: General - Fire barriers limit spread of fire
subsections.707.5: Continuity - Floor to underside of floor or roof deck above

903.title: Automatic Sprinkler Systems
903.summary: Requirements for when automatic sprinkler systems must be installed.

subsections.903.2: Where required - Group A, B, E, F, H, I, M, R, S occupancies
subsections.903.2.7: Group M - Mercantile occupancies over 12,000 sq ft

905.title: Standpipe Systems
905.summary: Requirements for standpipe systems in buildings.

subsections.905.3: Required installations - Buildings 3+ stories, stages, underground buildings, malls
subsections.905.3.1: Height - Required when floor level is more than 30 ft above or below fire dept access
subsections.905.4: Location of Class I standpipe hose connections - In stairways, horizontal exits, malls
subsections.905.5: Location of Class II standpipe hose connections
subsections.905.6: Location of Class III standpipe hose connections

907.title: Fire Alarm and Detection Systems
907.summary: Requirements for fire alarm systems, smoke detection, and notification.

subsections.907.2: Where required - Manual and automatic fire alarm systems by occupancy
subsections.907.2.1: Group A - Assembly occupancies exceeding 300 occupants
subsections.907.5: Notification appliances - Audible and visible alarms

909.title: Smoke Control Systems
909.summary: Requirements for engineered smoke control systems in specific building types.

subsections.909.1: Scope and purpose - Control smoke migration in means of egress
subsections.909.2: General design requirements - Rational analysis required
subsections.909.10: Equipment - Smoke control equipment and installation

1003.title: Means of Egress - General
1003.summary: General requirements for means of egress including exit access, exits, and exit discharge.

subsections.1003.2: Ceiling height - Min 7 ft 6 in. in means of egress
subsections.1003.3: Protruding objects - Max 4 in. projection into path when between 27 in. and 80 in. AFF
subsections.1003.4: Floor surface - Slip-resistant required in means of egress
subsections.1003.5: Elevation change - Ramp or stairway required for elevation changes exceeding 1/4 in.

1004.title: Occupant Load
1004.summary: Calculation of occupant load for determining egress requirements.

subsections.1004.1: Design occupant load - Per Table 1004.5 or actual, whichever is greater
subsections.1004.2: Areas without fixed seating - Calculated by floor area / occupant load factor
subsections.1004.3: Posting of occupant load - Required for assembly occupancies
subsections.1004.5: Table 1004.5 - Maximum floor area allowances per occupant (assembly 15 net sf, business 150 gross sf, educational 20 net sf, mercantile 60 gross sf)

1006.title: Number of Exits and Exit Access Doorways
1006.summary: Minimum number of exits based on occupant load.

subsections.1006.2: Minimum number of exits - 1 exit for OL ≤49 (with sprinklers in some cases), 2 exits for OL 1-500, 3 exits for OL 501-1000, 4 exits for OL >1000
subsections.1006.2.1: Single exit - Conditions allowing a single exit
subsections.1006.3: Exit access doorways - Number required from individual spaces

1010.title: Doors, Gates, and Turnstiles
1010.summary: Requirements for doors in means of egress.

subsections.1010.1: Doors - Size, type, and swing requirements
subsections.1010.1.1: Size of doors - Min 32 in. clear width, min 80 in. height
subsections.1010.1.2: Door swing - Direction of exit travel when serving OL ≥50
subsections.1010.1.3: Floor level at doors - Max 1/2 in. threshold height
subsections.1010.1.4: Landings at doors - Landing each side of door, length = door width

1011.title: Stairways
1011.summary: Requirements for stairways in means of egress.

subsections.1011.2: Width and capacity - Min 44 in. (36 in. if OL <50)
subsections.1011.3: Headroom - Min 6 ft 8 in. measured vertically from tread nosing
subsections.1011.5: Stairway risers and treads - Max 7 in. riser, min 11 in. tread
subsections.1011.7: Stairway landings - Required at top and bottom, length = width
subsections.1011.11: Handrails - Required on both sides, 34-38 in. height

1017.title: Exit Access Travel Distance
1017.summary: Maximum travel distance from any occupied point to the nearest exit.

subsections.1017.2: Table 1017.2 exit access travel distance - Without sprinklers: A/B/E/F/M/S/U = 200 ft, H-1 = 75 ft, H-2 = 100 ft, I-3 = 200 ft, R = 200 ft. With sprinklers: A/B/E/F/M/S/U = 250 ft, H = varies, I = 200-250 ft, R = 250 ft

1020.title: Corridors
1020.summary: Requirements for corridors used as exit access components.

subsections.1020.2: Width - Min 44 in. (36 in. if OL <50), 72 in. for Group I-2
subsections.1020.3: Dead-end corridors - Max 20 ft (50 ft with sprinklers)

1401.title: Exterior Walls - General
1401.summary: General provisions for exterior wall coverings and assemblies.

subsections.1401.1: Scope - Provisions governing exterior walls, wall coverings, and components
subsections.1401.2: Performance requirements - Weather resistance, structural, fire

1403.title: Exterior Wall Coverings - Performance Requirements
1403.summary: Weather protection and performance requirements for exterior wall coverings.

subsections.1403.2: Weather resistance - Water-resistive barrier required behind exterior wall covering
subsections.1403.5: Combustible materials on Type I and II construction - Limitations

1405.title: Exterior Wall Coverings - Installation
1405.summary: Installation requirements for various exterior wall covering materials.

subsections.1503.1: General - Roof drainage systems designed per applicable plumbing code
subsections.1503.4: Roof drainage overflow - Secondary drainage or scuppers required

1507.title: Roof Covering Materials and Application
1507.summary: Requirements for roof covering materials and their installation.

subsections.1507.2: Asphalt shingles - ASTM D3462, slope 2:12 minimum

1607.title: Live Loads
1607.summary: Minimum uniformly distributed and concentrated live loads for buildings.

subsections.Table 1607.1: Minimum uniformly distributed and concentrated live loads (offices 50 psf, residential 40 psf, corridors 100 psf, assembly 100 psf, storage 125-250 psf)

1608.title: Snow Loads
1608.summary: Design snow loads for buildings and structures per ASCE 7.

subsections.1608.2: Ground snow loads - Per Figure 1608.2 or site-specific study
subsections.1608.3: Design snow loads - Flat roof, sloped roof, drift, unbalanced per ASCE 7

1609.title: Wind Loads
1609.summary: Minimum design wind loads for buildings and structures per ASCE 7.

subsections.1609.3: Basic design wind speed - Per Figure 1609.3

1611.title: Rain Loads
1611.summary: Design rain loads for roofs considering ponding and drainage.

subsections.1611.1: Design rain loads - Based on static head depth to inlet of secondary drainage
subsections.1611.2: Ponding instability - Susceptible bays must be investigated for progressive deflection
subsections.1611.3: Controlled drainage - Roof designed for full depth of water to the inlet of secondary drainage

1612.title: Flood Loads
1612.summary: Requirements for buildings in flood hazard areas.

subsections.1612.1: General - Comply with ASCE 7 and ASCE 24

1613.title: Earthquake Loads
1613.summary: Seismic design requirements for buildings and structures.

subsections.1613.1: Scope - Every structure and portion thereof shall be designed per ASCE 7
subsections.1613.2: Definitions - Seismic Design Category, Base Shear, Importance Factor
subsections.1613.2.1: Mapped acceleration parameters - Ss and S1 from USGS maps
subsections.1613.2.5: Site class - Based on soil properties (A through F)
subsections.1613.3: Seismic Design Category determination - Based on SDS, SD1, Risk Category, and site class

1704.title: Special Inspections
1704.summary: General requirements for special inspections and testing during construction.

subsections.1704.1: General - Special inspections per this section and Section 1705
subsections.1704.2: Special inspector qualifications - ICC certified or PE/RA
subsections.1704.3: Statement of special inspections - Submitted by RDP before permit issuance
subsections.1704.5: Contractor responsibility - Provide access to work

1705.title: Required Special Inspections and Tests
1705.summary: Specific construction activities requiring special inspections.

subsections.1705.5: Wood construction - High-load diaphragms, metal-plate trusses
subsections.1705.8: Cast-in-place deep foundations (drilled shafts, auger piles)

1803.title: Geotechnical Investigations
1803.summary: When geotechnical investigations (soil reports) are required.

subsections.1803.1: General - Geotechnical investigation required where indicated
subsections.1803.2: Investigations required - Where Table 1809.7 soil capacity presumed values not used, questionable soil, fill, special design conditions
subsections.1803.3: Report requirements - Boring logs, recommended bearing, lateral pressure, settlement estimate, groundwater depth
subsections.1803.4: Liquefaction study - Required for SDC D, E, F

subsections.1808.1: General - Foundation design per geotechnical investigation and structural analysis
subsections.1808.2: Design for capacity - Designed for the most unfavorable load effects

1809.title: Shallow Foundations
1809.summary: Requirements for spread footings, mat foundations, and shallow foundations.

subsections.1809.1: General - Shallow foundations bear on competent soil or engineered fill
subsections.1809.4: Depth of footings - Min 12 in. below undisturbed ground, below frost line
subsections.1809.5: Footing size - Based on soil bearing capacity, min 12 in. width
subsections.1809.7: Table 1809.7 - Presumptive load-bearing values: rock 12,000 psf, gravel 3,000 psf, sand 2,000 psf, clay 1,500 psf, fill = engineering required
